programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Da Vinci Tech : Leonardo da Vinci remains intriguing 500 years after death; beyond great artist, he was remarkable scientist and inventor whose notebooks contained incredible machines from weapons to submarines, robots, and analogue computers with workable plans.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: '90s Tech : Technologies of the 1990s: the information superhighway; DVDs; TiVo; GPS; Google; Amazon distribution center; virtual pets; George Foreman's grill.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: BBQ Tech : Barbecue evolved from old-fashioned cooking into modern multi-billion dollar industry; explore famous cook-offs, established restaurants like Arthur Bryant's Kansas City, post-WWII backyard phenomenon, Weber kettle grills, and spicy sauce varieties.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Bible Tech : Arguably the most influential book ever written, the Bible provides a glimpse into the origins of ancient technology and its use to withstand the elements, build great structures, wage war, and conserve precious water.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Cowboy Tech : Today's cowboys blend Old West traditions with modern technology; examine 19th-century cattle industry boom, Spanish-derived saddles and ropes, barbed wire revolution, and modern computer chips, DNA evidence, and artificial insemination techniques.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Desert Tech : Close to 40% of Earth is classified as desert, but these environments offer hope through Middle East desalination, Las Vegas communities, Hoover Dam power, irrigation agriculture, solar energy, and technology enabling future Mars colonization.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Digi-Tech : DVD, CD, PDA, HDTV, PVR--used to be the ultimate in "gotta have it" gadgets and gizmos and "to die for" technology that populate a digital world of acronyms.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Home Tech : Take a peek into one of today's fully-automated homes that is so smart, the owner can operate everything from the home theater to the outdoor waterfall at a push of one button.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Winter Tech : The technology behind the Vancouver 2010 Winter Olympic Games, including the bobsled run, ski jump and speedskating.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Mummy Tech : Egyptian mummies speak from graves through state-of-the-art CT-scanning technology; explore 2,000-year-old child mummy, 70-day mummification process, King Tut's mysterious death investigation, and nitrogen-filled conservation cases preventing decay.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Pirate Tech : Pirates terrorized high seas since before recorded history; at their 1700s power height, they influenced nations through England-Spain rivalry while bringing maritime technology innovations including modified ships for speed and power enhancement.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Retro Tech : The evolution of technology; how things now considered obsolete worked such as the typewriter, VCR, analog TV, vinyl record, film camera and brick-sized mobile phone, as well as products that superseded them.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Swamp Tech : The assembly and surprising history behind the airboat; a wild ride on a big-tired swamp buggy; local delicacies that bring new meaning to acquired taste; a massive engineering project to divert the mighty Mississippi River; pythons and alligators.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Ben Franklin Tech : Benjamin Franklin, the oldest Founding Father, was the late 18th century's foremost scientist and greatest inventor; created Pennsylvania Stove, lightning rod, glass armonica for Mozart and Beethoven and anti-counterfeiting techniques.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Da Vinci Tech : Leonardo da Vinci remains intriguing 500 years after death; beyond great artist, he was remarkable scientist and inventor whose notebooks contained incredible machines from weapons to submarines, robots, and analogue computers with workable plans.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Harvesting : Cutting, digging, picking, stripping, shaking, and raking--whatever the crop, there's a custom machine to harvest it; it all began with handpicking and today it's often one man and one machine harvesting hundreds of acres in a single day.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Dinklescouts/I Dream of Cosmo : Dinklescouts/I Dream of Cosmo: Timmy wishes Mr. Dinkleberg would replace Dad as Squirrely Scout leader. Dad tries to regain Timmy’s respect and his leader position. Cosmo has amnesia and thinks he’s Dad’s genie.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Turner & Pooch/Dumbbell Curve : Turner & Pooch/Dumbbell Curve: Crocker seeks out a new source of magic from Timmy’s house. Realizing it’s Sparky, Crocker tries to turn him against Timmy. Crocker grades on a bell curve so Timmy wishes everyone was dumber than him.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: The Terrible Twosome/App Trap : The Terrible Twosome/App Trap: Poof goes through the terrible twos. Foop goes through the terrific twos. Timmy and his fairies have to survive Poof’s terrible twos.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Force of Nature/Viral Vidiots : Force of Nature/Viral Vidiots: At a park clean up, Timmy wishes the animals would pick up after themselves. The animals unite and launch an all-out attack on Timmy

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Scary GodCouple : Scary GodCouple: Foop becomes Vicky’s anti-fairy godparent. Vicky and Foop terrorize Timmy and the kids of Dimmsdale.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Two and a Half Babies/Anchor’s Away : Two and a Half Babies/Anchor's Away: Foop and Poof have to take care of an egg for a school project. Foop breaks it and swaps it with a dangerous one. Chet Ubetcha quits his job. Dad becomes the new news man.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Finding Emo/Dust Busters : Finding Emo/Dust Busters: Timmy likes a new girl at school. He wishes he was "emo" and stops caring about everything. Timmy and the fairies have to rid his home of fairy dust. The basement comes alive. Mom and Dad go to a fake spa.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: The Bored Identity/Country Clubbed : The Bored Identity/Country Clubbed: Dad is depressed because everyone thinks he is boring. Timmy wishes Dad’s life was like an action movie. Timmy and his parents get invited to a country club. Cosmo feuds with a gopher. Timmy wishes to be a socialite.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Dog Gone/Turner Back Time : Dog Gone/Turner Back Time: Dad refuses to sell Sparky to his boss so he makes Dad’s life miserable at work. Timmy and Sparky hatch a plan. Timmy wishes his ancestors had picked different careers. Timmy’s family becomes rich and spoiled.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Cosmonopoly/Hero Hound : Cosmonopoly/Hero Hound: Timmy, his dad, and fairies get stuck in a board game created by Cosmo. Timmy wishes Sparky would get a chance to be heroic. Dad thinks he can talk to animals.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: A Boy and His Dog-Boy/Crock Blocked : A Boy and His Dog-Boy/Crock Blocked: Timmy wishes Mr. Crocker would disappear. Crocker becomes invisible. Dad thinks the house is haunted. Timmy wishes Sparky was a human. Timmy becomes a dog and has to convince Sparky to change back to canine form.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Weirdos on a Train/Tons of Timmys : Weirdos on a Train/Tons of Timmys: Dad and Crocker make a deal. As a result, Crocker becomes best friends with Dinkleberg. Timmy wishes up 5000 Timmys for the Planet of the Dads. The Timmys kidnap Cosmo and Wanda.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Let Sleeper Dogs Lie/Cat-Astrophe : Let Sleeper Dogs Lie/Cat-astrophe: Crocker tries to trigger Sparky with a secret word. Catman confuses Sparky for his old arch-nemesis Dogman.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Lame Ducks/Perfect Nightmare : Lame Ducks/Perfect Nightmare: Dad organizes a neighborhood watch program to catch a neighborhood thief. Timmy wishes he had the perfect family in order to win a contest. Everyone goes crazy trying to be perfect.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Love at First Bark/Desperate Without Housewives : Love at First Bark/Desperate Without Housewives: Dad forbids Sparky from dating Dinkleberg’s new dog. Timmy wishes Dad would like the dog as much as Sparky. When Mom won’t stop nagging Timmy and Dad, Timmy wishes all women away for one day.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Jerk of All Trades/Snack Attack : Jerk of All Trades/Snack Attack: Timmy gets Jorgen fired. He helps Jorgen find a new job. Sparky makes his own magic dog treats. Crocker tries to buy one million of them in order to make a wish.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Turning into Turner/The Wand That Got Away : Turning into Turner/The Wand That Got Away: Using Timmy’s DNA, Crocker pretends to be Timmy from the future. Cosmo loses his wand and Timmy and the gang must help find it.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Stage Fright/Gone Flushin' : Stage Fright/Gone Flushin': Vicky decides to be an actress and makes Timmy help her land her first role. Dad accidentally flushes Timmy and the fairies down the toilet.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Fairly Old Parent : Fairly Old Parent: Poof finally becomes a fairy godparent. Unfortunately, it’s to Mrs. Crocker.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: School of Crock : School of Crock: Poof is going through "pooferty" and is finally learning to speak! Meanwhile, Mr. Crocker becomes a teacher at the Fairy Academy and tries to steal all the students’ magic!

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Dimmsdale Tales : Dimmsdale Tales: Dad takes the Squirrely Scouts on a camping trip and forgets all the supplies. Timmy tries to make the most of their trip by telling a collection of scary stories around a campfire.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: The Past and the Furious : The Past and the Furious: Cosmo and Wanda take Timmy on a trip back in time to meet some of their past Godkids. But things take a turn for the worst when Timmy interacts with the past and alters the future!

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: The Fairy Beginning : The Fairy Beginning: When Jorgen discovers that Cosmo never graduated from the Fairy Academy, he threatens to take away his job as Fairy Godparent. To solve the mystery of what happened, the gang looks through Cosmo’s memories of his teenage years.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Fairly Odd Fairy Tales : Fairly Odd Fairy Tales: In order to put a restless Poof to sleep, Wanda decides to read him a book of Fairy Tales. Only the "fairy tales" Wanda reads are the classic stories with a "Fairy Odd Parents" spin!

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Man’s Worst Friend : Man's Worst Friend: In order to make Timmy and his family miserable, Foop replaces Sparky with the Anti-Sparky. When that plan works, Foop decides to make all of Dimmsdale unhappy by replacing everyone's pet with an evil pet.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: The Big Fairy Share Scare : The Big Fairy Share Scare: Timmy finds out that he has to share his fairies with the new girl in school, Chloe Carmichael. But when Chloe realizes she can use her magic for good, she wishes everyone in Dimmsdale would share everything, and chaos ensues.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Whittle Me This/Mayor May Not : Whittle Me This/Mayor May Not: Catman thinks Timmy has been kidnapped. He mistakes Chloe for Catgirl and enlists her help to save Timmy. Timmy wishes Dad was Mayor, but Timmy gets tortured by the paparazzi and risks having his fairies discovered.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: Girly Squirrely/Birthday Battle: Transmorphers Versus Unicorns : Girly Squirrely/Birthday Battle: Transmorphers Versus Unicorns: Chloe joins the Squirrely Scouts and saves the troop by helping them get their first merit badge. Timmy and Chloe find out they have the same birthday and compete for the best party.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: The Fair Bears/Return of the L.O.S.E.R.S. : The Fair Bears/Return of the L.O.S.E.R.S.: Chloe’s favorite cartoon characters come to life and brainwash Timmy and Wanda. Timmy’s enemies learn that he is helpless without his fairies and try to destroy him when he’s weak.

programme.title: The Fairly OddParents
programme.desc: A Sash and a Rash/Fish Out of Water : A Sash and a Rash/Fish Out of Water: Chloe thinks she has a stress-caused rash and wishes to be a slacker, but Dimmsdale falls apart without her. Dad wants to catch a giant fish to win a trophy, but Chloe wishes all the fish could fight back.

programme.title: Ink Master
programme.desc: Weeding Out the Weak : The cutthroat battle for $100,000 and the title of Ink Master kicks off as 30 artists vie for one of 18 spots on Oliver Peck and Chris Nunez's teams.

programme.title: Ink Master

programme.desc: My Future Self n' Me : When a 32-year-old man claiming to be Stan from the future shows up in South Park, young Stan is forced to come to terms with the loser he will become.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: All About Mormons : A Mormon kid moves to South Park and it's up to Stan to kick his ass. But when Stan and his dad meet their new Mormon neighbors, they become fascinated with how genuinely nice they are.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Raisins : After Wendy breaks up with Stan the boys take him to 'Raisins,' a local restaurant known for its great chicken wings and hot girls.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: T.M.I. : Cartman throws a fit when the boys' penis sizes are posted on the school bulletin board, and is sent to anger management therapy.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: 1% : The 99% is ganging up on Eric Cartman.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Cartman Finds Love : The time has finally come for Cartman to let a special someone know exactly how he feels.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Raising The Bar : Cartman finally admits he's fat and immediately gets a mobility scooter.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Let Go, Let Gov : Cartman infiltrates the NSA and doesn’t like what he finds in his personal file.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: World War Zimmerman : Cartman sees Token as a threat to all humanity.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The Cissy : Randy is harboring a giant secret and the pressure is getting to him. Meanwhile, Cartman calls Stan a cissy.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: You're Not Yelping : Cartman considers himself the top on-line restaurant reviewer in South Park.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Cartman Gets An Anal Probe : While the boys are waiting for the school bus, Cartman explains the odd nightmare he had the previous night involving alien visitors.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Weight Gain 4000 : When Cartman's environmental essay wins a national contest, America's sweetheart, Kathie Lee Gifford, comes to South Park to present the award.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Roger Ebert Should Lay Off the Fatty Foods : Is the new planetarium a harmless place to learn about the solar system or the scene of a diabolical plot to control the minds of South Park's citizens?

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Cartman's Silly Hate Crime 2000 : Cartman is pursued by the FBI for committing a hate crime and lands in juvenile hall.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Cartman Joins NAMBLA : Cartman decides to seek friendship using the Internet and finds an older man who is more than willing to be his friend...and more.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Fat Camp : Cartman's family and friends have intervened and are forcing him to trim down. While Cartman's away, Kenny's star is on the rise when he gets his own reality TV show.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Scott Tenorman Must Die : After the other boys explain to Cartman that pubes don't count unless you grow your own, Cartman realizes he's been tricked by the school bully, Scott Tenorman.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Cartmanland : Cartman inherits a million dollars from his grandmother and fulfills his lifelong dream of owning his own amusement park: Cartmanland!

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: AWESOM-O : Cartman plans to learn all of Butters' innermost secrets and then use them against him, by pretending to be Butters' new best friend, a robot named AWESOM-O.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Cartman's Incredible Gift : After sustaining a severe head injury, Cartman appears to have the power to see into the future. South Park detectives are quick to enlist his help in cracking unsolved murder cases.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The Death of Eric Cartman : Cartman is sure he's dead and Butters is sure that Eric Cartman's ghost is haunting him. Together they attempt to make amends to all the people Cartman has wronged over the years.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Ginger Kids : After the sudden on-set of the disease Gingervitis, Cartman rallies all other ginger kids to rise up and assume their role as the master race.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Manbearpig : Al Gore warns the school about the threat of Manbearpig.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Tsst : When Cartman's mom realizes she can't control her son anymore, she gets help from an expert. The "Dog Whisperer" may have what it takes but Eric Cartman's not going down without a fight.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Go God Go : South Park Elementary faces strong opposition to the topic of evolution. Cartman's too busy to notice as he plans to propel himself into the future on the precise release date of the newest, hottest game console.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Go God Go XII : Eric Cartman has frozen himself in an attempt to make his three-week wait for a Nintendo Wii pass quickly. A freak accident landed him over 500 years in the future and now, he's stuck in a Godless world on the brink of war with no Nintendo Wii.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Cartman Sucks : When his "ultimate plan" to embarrass Butters backfires, Cartman struggles to keep his classmates from seeing a compromising photograph. Meanwhile, Butters is sent to a special camp where they "Pray the Gay Away."

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Le Petit Tourette : Cartman discovers the joys of having Tourette’s syndrome. Drunk with the power of saying whatever he wants without getting in trouble for it, he lines up national TV coverage to take advantage of his new life with no filters.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Tonsil Trouble : Cartman finds himself fighting for his life after a routine tonsillectomy goes wrong. When Kyle becomes infected with Cartman's ailment, the two won't stop for anything as they search for a cure that will reverse their otherwise certain mortality.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The Coon : The Coon rises from the trash and takes his place as a lone vigilante who wipes out crime in the town of South Park.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Fatbeard : Cartman's dream of living the life of a pirate will come true if he can just get to Somalia.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: A History Channel Thanksgiving : The boys are getting close to discovering the truth about the first Thanksgiving.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Taming Strange : When Ike hits puberty, he and Kyle start to grow apart. To save their relationship, Kyle takes Ike to see a live performance of Yo Gabba Gabba.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: #REHASH : Kyle just wants to play video games with his little brother. But, when Ike doesn’t want to play with him anymore, Kyle is afraid that the next generation is passing him by.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: #HappyHolograms : Cartmaan Bra is trending as the country prepares to watch the biggest Holiday Spectacular ever.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: You're Not Yelping : Cartman considers himself the top on-line restaurant reviewer in South Park.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: An Elephant Makes Love To A Pig : Kyle's mom won't let him keep his new pet, an elephant because it's so huge. So the boys turn turn to the Dr. Mephesto to genetically engineer a smaller elephant.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Mr. Hankey, the Christmas Poo : While South Park Elementary is attempting to stage a non-denominational holiday play that won't offend (or entertain) anyone, Kyle checks himself into the South Park mental asylum.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Mecha-Streisand : The boys' discovery of a prehistoric relic spawns a monster that threatens to destroy South Park and the world.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Ike's Wee Wee : Mr. Mackey, the school counselor, is fired and turns to drugs and alcohol. Meanwhile, when the boys find out what it means to be circumcised they try to save Ike from his Bris.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Chinpokomon : Stan, Kyle, Cartman and Kenny are caught up in the latest fad from Japan: Chinpoko Mon!

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Tooth Fairy's Tats 2000 : When Cartman discovers the Tooth Fairy is paying a premium price for his lost teeth, he and the boys seize the opportunity to make some cash.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Cherokee Hair Tampons : Kyle needs a kidney transplant and Cartman is discovered to be the perfect donor. Cartman gladly offers his kidney to Kyle -- for the price of $10 million dollars.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The Wacky Molestation Adventure : To get back at his parents for not letting him go to a concert, Kyle tells the police that his parents molested him. Soon, the whole town is free of adults and divided into two rival cities.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The Entity : Mr. Garrison gets fed up with long lines, delayed flights and the airline industry in general and invents his own transportation device.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Toilet Paper : Cartman convinces the boys to "toilet paper" their teacher's house, but Kyle is overwhelmed with guilt.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Casa Bonita : Kyle chooses Stan, Kenny, and Butters to celebrate his birthday at Casa Bonita. When Cartman finds out he's not invited, he arranges for Butters to conveniently go "missing."

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Mr. Garrison's Fancy New Vagina : As Mr. Garrison enjoys his new womanly attributes after his sex change, the rest of the town gets in touch with their inner feelings too.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Free Willzyx : The boys try to rescue their new friend Willzyx, a talking space whale, from the Denver Sea Park, and with the help of MASA (Mexican Aeronautica y Spacia Administracion) get him home to the moon for $200.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Smug Alert! : Kyle and his family are moving to San Francisco. The only way Stan can get his best friend back is to convince everyone to start driving hybrid cars.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Imaginationland : When the entire contents of the world’s imagination lay open before them, Stan and Kyle step right in. Back in South Park, Cartman swears he’s seen a leprechaun.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Imaginationland Episode II : Stan and Kyle are being held in the bowels of the Pentagon until they tell the government how they got into Imaginationland. Meanwhile, Cartman simply won’t rest until he finds Kyle and gets him to make good on their bet to suck his balls.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Imaginationland Episode III : Inside Imaginationland, Stan and Butters engage in the battle of their lives as they fight the army of evil imaginary forces. Meanwhile, Cartman goes all the way to the Supreme Court to get Kyle to pay up on their bet.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Guitar Queer-O : Stan and Kyle are hooked on Guitar Hero. Unfortunately, Stan’s superior skills on the video game damage his friendship with Kyle.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The List : The girls in the fourth grade class have made a secret list that rates every boy’s looks from cutest to ugliest. When the boys steal the list, they are completely unprepared to deal with the results.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Margaritaville : Randy steps forward with a solution to fix the desperate state of the economy.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Whale Whores : Stan takes action to stop the Japanese from killing the world’s whales and dolphins.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Sexual Healing : The latest in scientific testing reveals that some of the boys at South Park Elementary have a sex addiction problem.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: It's A Jersey Thing : New Jersey is taking over the nation one state at a time. Randy and the boys take a stand as the Jerseyites approach South Park.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: HUMANCENTiPAD : Kyle is intimately involved in the development of a revolutionary new product called HumancentiPad.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Crack Baby Athletic Association : Kyle gets in on the ground floor of Cartman’s latest business venture, The Crack Baby Athletic Association.

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: X Marks The Murder Parts 1 : Soft music, candles, and a lifeless body dressed in lovely lingerie. These are the trademarks of 'The Casanova Killer,' a serial murderer who leaves only a stain of blood near the heart, and the sole of a foot marred by an 'X,' as forensic evidence.

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.desc: An Explosive Murder : A terrorist bombing ignites an undercover investigation involving an ambitious cop.

programme.desc: Murder By The Busload : Dr. Sloan finds a murder victim among the trauma patients from a major bus accident.

programme.desc: The ABC's Of Murder : Dr. Sloan and a headstrong attorney (Piper Laurie) investigate a drive-by shooting at a high school.

programme.desc: Sky Cops : In the West of London, police are scouring marsh land for a wanted man.

programme.title: Sky Cops

programme.title: Homicide: Hours to Kill
programme.desc: Bad Blood : Fifteen-year-old twins come home to find their mother dead in their bathtub; their testimonies lead the investigation, but one detail highlights the troubling truth.

programme.title: Homicide: Hours to Kill
programme.desc: Behind Closed Doors : Florida detectives investigate the death of Karen Pannell and the bloody message left behind; a pizza delivery holds a clue to the mystery.

programme.title: Homicide: Hours to Kill
programme.desc: Million Dollar Murder : Andrew Kissel, a confessed fraudster, is found stabbed to death in the basement of his Connecticut home; detectives must unravel every detail from the previous 24 hours and, in doing so, come up with a long list of suspects.

programme.title: Homicide: Hours to Kill
programme.desc: Monster at the Gates : A Jacksonville firefighter returns home to find his wife's dead body in their bedroom; when he admits to infidelity, he becomes the prime suspect, but investigators are led to the real killer via a stolen car.

programme.title: Homicide: Hours to Kill
programme.desc: Girl Interrupted : An 18-year-old woman from Staten Island meets her demise after entering the dangerous world of internet dating, and the media spins her secrets into scandals.

programme.title: Homicide: Hours to Kill
programme.desc: Three's a Crowd : An exploration of how the shooter of Desiree Sunford, a teacher, was brought to justice and how he nearly evaded the law altogether.

programme.title: Homicide: Hours to Kill

programme.title: Homicide: Hours to Kill

programme.title: Homicide: Hours to Kill
programme.desc: An Eye for An Eye : When an ambitious and successful woman is shot dead in the garage of her suburban Texas home, investigators have many suspects to choose from, including her son, her ex-husband, and two admirers.

programme.title: Homicide: Hours to Kill
programme.desc: Somebody Knows Something : Even after a decade, the witnesses to Deborah Boothby's murder are still too afraid to talk.

programme.title: Homicide: Hours to Kill
programme.desc: Secrets In Suburbia : A community is in distress when a woman is found clubbed to death in her home, and investigators attempt to reconstruct her final hours.

programme.title: Homicide: Hours to Kill
programme.desc: A Nightmare Before Christmas : After a man is gunned down in his home just days before Christmas, detectives hunt for a mystery murderer with a sinister motive.

programme.title: Homicide: Hours to Kill
programme.desc: Deranged and Deadly : A woman is killed and her body left in her son's bed, but investigators look deeper into her murder when her son is stabbed 18 times to the chest upon his return home.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Another Mother - September 30, 1981 : Sam (Scott Bakula) comes to the aid of a divorced mother whose socially awkward teen-age son may make a tragic mistake if he succumbs to peer pressure.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: All-Americans - November 6, 1962 : Sam (Scott Bakula) leaps into the life of a 1962 teen-age football star who desperately tries to prevent a teammate (Richard Coca) from throwing the big game. With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Her Charm - September 26, 1973 : Sam (Scott Bakula) becomes an FBI agent who is assigned to guard a woman (Teri Austin) targeted for assassination by her drug-dealing ex-boss. With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Freedom - November 22, 1970 : Sam (Scott Bakula) leaps into the life of a young American Indian in 1970, outrunning the law to let his ailing grandfather (Frank Sotonoma ``Gray Wolf'' Salsedo) die with dignity. Also guest stars Gloria Hayes.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Goodnight, Dear Heart - November 9, 1957 : Sam (Scott Bakula) becomes a mortician in 1958 who is strangely obsessed with the suicide of a teen-age German girl. With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Pool Hall Blues - September 4, 1954 : Sam (Scott Bakula) leaps into the life of an aging pool shark in 1954, faced with the match of his lifetime. Shari Headley guest stars. With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Leaping in Without a Net - November 18, 1958 : An acrophobic Sam (Scott Bakula) becomes a 1958 trapeze artist preparing for a death-defying stunt. With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Maybe Baby - March 11, 1963 : Sam (Scott Bakula) is a burly nightclub bouncer who is helping a stripper (Julie Brown) rescue an infant from its abusive father.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Sea Bride - June 3, 1954 : Sam (Scott Bakula) finds himself on an ocean liner in 1954 as a desperate young man trying to win back the heart of his ex-wife. Also stars Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: MIA - April 1, 1969 : Al (Dean Stockwell) attempts to adjust his past through Sam (Scott Bakula), who has become an undercover cop in 1969.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: The Leap Home, Pt. I - November 25, 1969 : Sam (Scott Bakula) realizes a leap-long wish and becomes himself in 1969, tempted to change the course of his family's future. David Newsom guest stars. With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: The Leap Home, Pt. II - April 7, 1970 : Sam (Scott Bakula) leaps into the life of an American soldier in Vietnam whose field officer is Tom Beckett (David Newsom) -Sam's brother.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: Leap of Faith - August 19, 1963 : Sam (Scott Bakula) is a priest in 1963 whose fellow pastor (Sandy McPeak) finds his own life in jeopardy because he knows a killer's identity. With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: One Strobe Over the Line - June 15, 1965 : In 1963, Sam (Scott Bakula) is a fashion photographer trying to prevent a model's overbearing agent (Susan Anton) from pushing her over the brink. With Dean Stockwell.

programme.title: Quantum Leap
programme.desc: The Boogieman - October 31, 1964 : Sam (Scott Bakula) is brought back to Halloween 1964 as a horror novelist who has his own share of scary experiences, including an encounter with the devil. With Dean Stockwell.

channel.display-name: The Dog Whisperer with Caesar Millan

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Pack Leaders in Training : Young fans Jessica and Rachel join Cesar as he deals with a tiny dog with an outsized personality and a nervous pit bull mix afraid of the sea.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Hounds From Hell : Cesar helps owners of rescue dogs including a terrier mix that screams and a Belgian Tervuren scared of her own home; Cesar helps a family adopting their first dog.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Horrible Hounds of the U.K. : Cesar travels to the U.K. to help troubled dog owners with a territorial street dog and a dalmatian whose personality can turn ugly.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: London Calling : British problem dogs including wild German Shepherds, a dog that must be muzzled by court order, and an English cocker spaniel that hates cars.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Cesar Conquers London : Problem dogs in the U.K. including two dogs that spent most of their lives in Africa, and a barking Jack Russell terrier mix.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Battle of Britain : Cesar's English adventure ends with an aggressive klee kai, a collie mix that hates walks, and a hyper Labrador.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Jersey Shore Dogs : Cesar brings discipline to four chaotic Chinese crested dogs, an over-energetic mastiff mix and two unruly miniature dachshunds.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Anger Management : Cesar helps the lead singer of Rooney with his aggressive pit bull, while writer and producer Paul needs help with his two Boston terriers.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Playboy Playmates : Playboy Playmates, Miss February 2010 is having trouble with her Chichuahua, while Orange County, Miss September 2011 with her terrier pug mix.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Bad Dogs of Comedy : Cesar helps comedians Skyler Stone, Ralphie May and Paul Rodriguez get to grips with their unruly hounds.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Fear Factor : Cesar helps dogs overcome their fears including a whippet mix who is blind and deaf, a labradoodle that fears the ocean, and a cocker spaniel that fears all humans.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Daytona Dogs : Cesar meets NASCAR driver Kevin Harvick to help with his snappy Chihuahua and an aggressive Pekingese called Gizmo.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Hollywood Hounds : Cesar helps fitness guru Jillian Michaels with her aggressive Chihuahua, while "Frasier" star Kelsey Grammer needs help house-training his dogs.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Bella and Jordan : A couple fear their border terrier is untrainable; an excitable English bulldog causes trouble for its owner.

programme.title: Dog Whisperer
programme.desc: Sunshine and Teddy : An owner worries that her Doberman suffers from separation anxiety; a yellow lab has a problem with biting.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Crème Fraiche : Randy’s obsession with the Food Network forces Sharon to explore a new interest of her own.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Royal Pudding : The Prince of Canada is about to take a Princess and Ike is obsessed with the Royal Wedding.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Crack Baby Athletic Association : Kyle gets in on the ground floor of Cartman’s latest business venture, The Crack Baby Athletic Association.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Taming Strange : When Ike hits puberty, he and Kyle start to grow apart. To save their relationship, Kyle takes Ike to see a live performance of Yo Gabba Gabba.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Freemium Isn't Free : Stan is addicted to the new Terrance and Phillip mobile game.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Where My Country Gone? : Garrison wants to build a wall to keep out all of the undocumented immigrants.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: SUPER HARD PCness : It’s never been more challenging to be a PC Principal.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Splatty Tomato : The children of South Park claim to have seen Mr. Garrison lurking around town. The townspeople are angry that the president is scaring their children.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Death : Grandpa's sole birthday wish is for Stan to take part in his assisted suicide. Meanwhile, Mrs. Broflovski has organized a protest against the boys' favorite TV show, "Terrance and Phillip."

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Starvin' Marvin : Mistaking Cartman for a starving African child, government authorities send him to Ethiopia where he runs into Sally Struthers.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Terrance and Phillip in Not Without My Anus : Terrance and Phillip must save Terrance's Little Daughter, Sally, and all of Canada from an evil dictator.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Chickenpox : The kids' parents only have their best interests at heart when they arrange for Stan, Kyle and Cartman to be exposed to the chickenpox virus.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Cow Days : South Park's 14th Annual "Cow Days" rodeo and carnival is here and the boys are determined to win Terrance and Phillip dolls.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Spookyfish : When Sharon Marsh's Aunt Flo makes a monthly visit, she brings a mysterious pet fish for Stan.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Merry Christmas Charlie Manson! : Dinner with Cartman's family takes a bizarre twist when Uncle Howard shows up after breaking out of prison with the help of his cellmate, Charlie Manson.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Timmy 2000 : When Timmy is diagnosed with Attention Deficit Disorder, it triggers a wave of prescription drug abuse at South Park Elementary.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Terrance and Phillip: Behind the Blow : When the boys discover Terrance and Philip have called it quits, they'll go to any lengths to reunite the duo and recruit them for South Park's Earth Day festivities.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The New Terrance and Phillip Movie Trailer : All the kids in South Park are glued to the TV, wild with anticipation to see the first trailer for the latest Terrance and Phillip movie.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The Simpsons Already Did It : Professor Chaos is frustrated when he realizes all of his diabolical plans to wreak havoc on South Park have already been done by "The Simpsons."

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: It's Christmas in Canada : The Brovlofski family is dealt a devastating blow when Ike's Canadian birth parents show up unexpectedly. When the townspeople decide to forego Christmas gifts and take up a collection to get Ike home to South Park, the boys are distraught.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Canada On Strike : The head of the World Canadian Bureau leads the country into a long and painful strike and the responsibility of brokering a settlement rests with the boys.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Eat, Pray, Queef : Someone plays an April Fool’s joke on the boys and it doesn’t go over well.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Menage a Troi : When Deanna's mother spurns advances made by a Ferengi Daimon, he takes it upon himself to kidnap the two of them, along with Commander Riker and steal them away aboard his ship.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Sins of the Father : A Klingon commander boards the Enterprise in an officer exchange program initiated by Starfleet, much to the chagrin of the crew.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Allegiance : Picard is kidnapped and held with three different aliens and meanwhile replaced with a replica as impostor captain.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Captain's Holiday : After mediating a difficult peace agreement, Captain Picard is encouraged to take a much needed rest on a vacationing planet, where he's visited by a strange race from the future, in search of a dangerous weapon.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Tin Man : The Enterprise reaches out to an alien being while dancing around with the Romulans, who want to attack it in an act of vengeance.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Hollow Pursuits : A shy member of the "Enterprise" crew becomes addicted to the holodeck, where relationships are easier there than in real life, when his knowledge is needed in engineering.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: The Most Toys : When transporting a dangerous material to the Enterprise, Data is kidnapped and the crew is led to believe he is dead when the shuttlecraft he was piloting explodes enroute.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Sarek : Legendary Federation ambassador Sarek visits the Enterprise to conclude peace talks with a race called the Legarans.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Menage a Troi : When Deanna's mother spurns advances made by a Ferengi Daimon, he takes it upon himself to kidnap the two of them, along with Commander Riker and steal them away aboard his ship.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Transfigurations : The Enterprise finds a deserted planet where a ship has crashed, and, with it, the lone survivor with no memory, but extraordinary healing powers.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: The Best of Both Worlds (Part 1) : Responding to a distress call on one of the Federation's outer-most colonies, the Enterprise arrives...only to find a big hole in the ground where the town used to be, and discovers the Borg are behind the attack.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: The Best of Both Worlds (Part 2) : Commander Riker had no choice as acting captain but fire full force on the Borg starship, despite Picard being on board of it, altered to a Borg calling himself Locutus, both ships are damaged.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Family : After defeating the Borg, the crew of the Enterprise experiences shore leave in various ways. Captain Picard's return to his family's vineyard in France has some extraordinary repercussions following his time spent as a member of the Borg collective.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Brothers : After an accident aboard the Enterprise leaves one of its children in grave danger, Data commandeers the Enterprise, driven to take him to an unknown origin, where an interesting figures awaits.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Suddenly Human : On a rescue mission to an alien shipwrecked training mission, they discover one of its trainees to be a human.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Original Series
programme.desc: Is There In Truth No Beauty? : Miranda Jones, a telepath who studied mental disciplines on Vulcan, arrives with Ambassador Kolos, a Medusan.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Original Series
programme.desc: Spectre Of The Gun : When coming to a xenophobic isolationist planet, Captain Kirk and his landing party are punished for trespassing.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Original Series
programme.desc: Day Of The Dove : When the Enterprise brings aboard Klingon prisoners, an alien entity pits both sides against each other in an ever-escalating struggle.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Original Series
programme.desc: For The World Is Hollow And I Have Touched The Sky : At the same time McCoy discovers that he has a year to live, the U.S.S. Enterprise encounters the asteroid Yonada which is determined to be artificially propelled.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Original Series
programme.desc: Spock's Brain : The crew of the Enterprise pursues a mysterious woman who has abducted Spock's brain.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Original Series
programme.desc: The Enterprise Incident : Captain Kirk becomes increasingly erratic and orders the Enterprise into Romulan space. Here, the ship is captured by a beautiful Romulan commander.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Original Series
programme.desc: The Paradise Syndrome : Kirk loses his memory and is proclaimed a God while trapped on a planet whose inhabitants are descended from Northwestern Native Americans.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Original Series
programme.desc: And The Children Shall Lead : The Enterprise reaches a Federation colony where the adults have all killed themselves but the children play without care.

programme.title: Nash Bridges
programme.desc: Nash Bridges : Don Johnson is Nash Bridges, a San Francisco police investigator who deserves his reputation as the city’s top cop. Nash relies on his instincts, a keen sense of humor and a lot of charm to work his magic on the streets of San Francisco.

programme.desc: Nash Bridges : Don Johnson is Nash Bridges, a San Francisco police investigator who deserves his reputation as the city’s top cop. Nash relies on his instincts, a keen sense of humor and a lot of charm to work his magic on the streets of San Francisco.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: My Family Slaughtered For My Daughter's Teenage Love: Can A Father Forgive Pt. 2 : Dr. Phil continues his exclusive interview with Erin Caffey, sentenced to two life terms in prison plus 25 years for murders of her mother and brothers in 2008. Part 2

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: The Honeymoon's Over : Do couples give up too easily on marriage? More newlyweds, married only a few months, are pulling off their wedding bands and calling it quits. Dr. Phil's guests haven't even been married a year, and are already wondering if they made the right decision.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: A Father Accused: Who's Telling The Truth? : Horrific allegations are tearing a family apart. Todd, ex girlfriend Latia & mom Diana tell their versions of sexual misconduct. Todd says he will do anything to prove his innocence. Dr. Phil calls in a polygraph examiner, with shocking results.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: A Questionable Will & A Suspect Twin: Is This The Craziest Catfish Story Of Them All Pt. 2 : In one of the most bizarre love stories to come across Dr. Phil’s stage, now Dr. Phil uncovers a massive secret that changes everything with Ryan and Suzanne. Here is the conclusion to a fascinating tale of lies, deception and emotional extortion! Part 2

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: An Unspeakable Accusation: My Daughter Believes I Molested My Granddaughter : Terrie says daughter Kelly accused her of molesting her 5-yr-old granddaughter. Terrie says Kelly attended a week-long desert retreat that affected her personality: Kelly says she saw aliens on the retreat who may have abducted, raped & impregnated her.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: A Father Accused, A Mother Blamed, The Truth Revealed Pt. 2 : Dr. Phil continues with Megan, her ex-boyfriend, Andy and his fiancee Caitlin, who have presented Dr. Phil with evidence supporting their allegation that it is Megan's own lifestyle & choices keeping her out of her son's life. Who is telling the truth?

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Summer Wells Disappearance: What Do The Parents Know? Pt. 2 : There have been no answers to the whereabouts of Summer Wells, 5, missing since 2021. Parents Don & Candus say it backfired by requesting the public's help. In this exclusive first national TV interview, Dr. Phil digs deeper into the story.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Ask Dr. Phil : It's Ask Dr. Phil day. Is it an attractive woman's responsibility to tone down her looks on the job? Debrahlee made headlines saying she was fired for being too pretty. She says she was told her figure, pencil skirts & 3-inch heels were too distracting.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Absentee Mom, Clueless Dad : John says his ex-wife, Laurie, abandoned their children. John admits his boys disrespect his authority, with one son punching him in the face. As Laurie denies deserting her children, we hear from the three boys: is it clueless parenting or unruly kids?

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Daughter's Boyfriend Has Been Arrested For Domestic Battery Twice But She Refuses To Leave Him : Keirsten claims her boyfriend, Mike, has slammed her face into the car radio and repeatedly hit her in the head, giving her several black eyes and a bloody lip. Although Mike's been arrested twice for domestic battery, she loves him and he's a great dad.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Charlie Meets His "Fiancee" Face-To-Face Pt. 2 : Dr. Phil continues with Brittany who says dad Charlie is being scammed by his online fiancée "Stephanie" & her roommate "Carina." Charlie hopes to get married when they meet in person. The real women in the photos are on stage & Charlie goes face-to-face!

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Misbehaving Moms? : Leah says mom Dora appears sweet but she's actually a narcissist, hypochondriac, drama queen & liar. Can this mother-daughter find common ground and rebuild? Plus Carrie says mom Paula, 61, has been meeting strangers online having sex. Dr. Phil weighs in.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Multiple Restraining Orders And Fake Births: Brittany Speaks Pt. 2 : After former friends Randi & Haley tell Brittany's history of harassing men & faking pregnancies, Dr. Phil talks to Brittany and asks her to prove she is pregnant. What sent Brittany storming off stage? See the backstage conversation with Dr. Phil.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Bully Dads : They threaten, scream, intimidate and even bite. They are not tantrum-throwing tots ... they are bullying dads!

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Talk To Dr. Phil Or We're Done Pt. 1 : For the past 2 years Nancy has been in an online relationship with "Shaun" she says is an honest and loving man. Her son Darcy says Nancy doesn't even know what he looks like! Nancy is adamant she is not being scammed in this catfish story of online love.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Pregnant And Out Of Control: Will The Baby Be Born Addicted Pt. 1 : Kristin says she’s desperate for an intervention for her daughter, Sara, 18, seven months pregnant who admits to smoking cigarettes and marijuana daily and using cocaine and heroin. Drug test results, plus Dr. Phil uncovers shocking childhood details. Par

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Finding Allene & Mollie Pt. 3 : The tense family drama continues. Dr. Phil sent Private Investigator Harold Copus thousands of miles through three countries including the jungles of Belize to find two sisters, Allene and Mollie, who were abducted by their father one year ago. Part 3

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: A Second Schizophrenic Child Or A Misdiagnosing Mom: Can Bodhi Be Helped Pt. 2 : Dr. Phil continues discussion with exes Michael and Susan, as well as Susan’s new husband, Cory, about the care of 11-year-old Bodhi, who Susan insists is experiencing schizophrenia, just like Michael and Susan’s 16-year-old daughter, Janni. Part 2

programme.title: Love Island Australia
programme.desc: Love Island Australia : Matthew and Cartier adjust to their coupling. The remaining couples marvel that they've made it to the final week. A yoga session leads to embarrassment for Josh. Jessie worries that she's not enough for Todd.

programme.title: Love Island Australia
programme.desc: Love Island Australia : The islanders are in the pitfalls of parenting as the babies arrive. The Australian public decides on another dumping.

programme.title: Love Island Australia
programme.desc: Love Island Australia : The islanders are put to the test when they answer questions about their partners during Dunk in Love. One couple makes their relationship official.

programme.title: Love Island Australia
programme.desc: Love Island Australia : Glamorous singles are sent to a villa. The audience decide who stays and who goes.

programme.title: Love Island Australia
programme.desc: Love Island Australia : It's finale time, and after a last sunrise breakfast together, the couples prepare to face a live audience and a live public vote. But which of them has done enough to be crowned the 2019 champions.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Love Island : The first eleven candidates get to know each other. Chloe recognises one of the other residents. Jessica and Montana have something in common. Marcel has been a pop star in the past.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Love Island : The brand new series of Love Island continues as the hot young singles settle into the villa for a summer of dating, flirting and fun in the sun. Is history repeating for Montana and Jessica and are Olivia and Sam an item?

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Love Island : The girls get a welcome surprise when two new lads arrive on the island. As romances blossom, Chloe and Camilla find themselves sidelined. Dom and Jessica grow closer, while Kem prepares to make a play for Amber.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Love Island : The girls chat about which boys have caught their attention, and the boys are concerned about their new competition.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Love Island : As the first week of fun in the sun builds to a climax, Chloe and Camilla face a heart-wrenching dilemma as re-coupling day looms. Meanwhile, a pillow fight between Kem and Amber quickly turns sour.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: The Weekly Hot List : It is time to catch up on an unforgettable first week in the villa, with loads of unseen action and all the best moments crammed into one jam-packed hour.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Love Island : The boys and girls reflect on the previous boy who was kicked out. When two new girls arrive in the villa, tensions begin to rise between the existing groups.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Love Island : Chris plays a dangerous game. Olivia prepares to pie Sam. Camilla sheds her inhibitions during a kissing challenge. Marcel and Gabby get closer.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Love Island : The villa plays host to its most dramatic evening yet as Jessica's birthday party turns sour. Chris bins Chloe and Sam calls out Chris for his backstabbing behaviour. Later, Dom and Jessica escape to the hideaway.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Love Island : A debate over feminism leaves a question mark over Camilla and Jonny's relationship. Will Camilla accept Jonny's apology? Meanwhile, Marcel and Gabby enjoy their first kiss and Montana and Chloe worry about their future in the villa.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Defensless : A woman Navy ensign shoots and kills a Turkish colonel; she claims self-defense.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: With Intent to Die : With help from Harm, Mac, Harriet, and Bud, Admiral Chedwiggen investigates the apparent suicide of his mentor, Admiral Clancy, who allegedly shot himself while on a hunting trip. Harm and company ultimately uncover who the killer was.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Father's Day : Mac and Harm are assigned the task of defending a young Marine whose frustration at being unable to wrest custody of his son has led to his being court-martialed for dereliction of duty.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Tiger, Tiger : Harm takes Josh on a weekend cruise for Navy families aboard a frigate. When the frigate is taken over by terrorists who intend to fire missiles at Cuba, Harm and Bud take matters into their own hands to stop the evil plot.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: The Imposter : Harm is held captive by Palmer, a renegade government agent with whom he had crossed swords a few months ago. While Palmer prepares a life mask so he can pass for Harm, he explains his plans to assassinate the key witness in the trial.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: The Return of Jimmy Blackhorse : An Army forensic pathologist joins forces with Harm, Mac, Bud and Harriet as they try to verify that newly unearthed human remains are those of a heroic Navajo "code-talker" who had been missing since WWII.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Vanished : Concerns regarding the mysterious disappearance of a fully armed Navy F-14 during a storm lead Harm's investigation to consider such diverse scenarios as pilot suicide, terrorism, the Bermuda Triangle curse, or UFO interference.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Against All Enemies : Harm, Mac and Bud board an aircraft carrier in the Sea of Japan to investigate the accidental shooting down of a North Korean civilian airliner by Navy jets.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Impact : Harm and Bud investigate the crash of a Marine helicopter whose dead pilot is being blamed for the incident. They discover the chopper seems to have been hit by some form of UFO. Government security officials want to maintain a cover-up at all costs.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: People V. Rabb : Harm is arrested by the FBI for the murder of Konaplanik, a Russian who was selling Harm supposedly accurate information about his missing father's whereabouts. The killer turns out to be Col. Tomashevsky, a Russian agent.

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Defensless : A woman Navy ensign shoots and kills a Turkish colonel; she claims self-defense.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Three Hearts : When an undercover NCIS agent is suspected of working for the smuggler he's supposed to be investigating, the team takes him into custody to find his true intentions.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: One More Chance : When software for a new, unmanned aerial vehicle goes missing and the lead engineer's 10-year-old daughter disappears, Sarah is convinced the two are related.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Exposure : The team investigates when a news reporter names the people responsible for a deadly explosion at a charity event sponsored by the military.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Deep Trouble Part 1 : The team discovers the cartel's plan to transport cocaine to the U.S. from Colombia using a submarine; Hetty must go to Washington.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Deep Trouble Part 2 : When Callen and Sam are trapped in an armed submarine that terrorists have aimed at an aircraft carrier in San Diego, the team must find them and prevent the attack.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Inelegant Heart : The team discovers that one of their own is compromised when they investigate the murder of a navy contractor involved in illegal transactions of confidential data.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Praesidium : The team takes action to protect one of their own while being interrogated by the Department of Justice.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: The 3rd Choir : When the team learns that someone from their past is targeting one of their own, they desperately search the city.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Black Budget : Callen and Sam head to Mexico to track down the only employee who managed to escape a professional shooting at the Department of Defense.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Seal Hunter : When Sam is arrested for murdering a woman, Callen and Granger work to clear his name.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Leipei : When a man is murdered by a small drone, the team discovers his connection to a terrorist group that is planning another local attack.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: The Grey Man : Kensi and Deeks go undercover to investigate the murder of a homeless man who was a former marine

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Traitor : After Granger is poisoned by the mole and Eric is one of the seven suspects, Hetty orders a lockdown while everyone is investigated.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Reign Fall : Sam reflects on his own upbringing and relationship with his father when the team investigates the murders of marines who have sons in military academies.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Humbug : The team investigates a burglary at a cyber-security company but Callen must steer clear after Hetty reveals that his girlfriend, Joelle, is one of the lead witness.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: Spiral : While Callen is undercover in the mail room of an office building to investigate an arms dealer, it is taken over by terrorists and Callen becomes a hostage.

programme.title: NCIS Los Angeles
programme.desc: In The Line Of Duty : After U.S. Ambassador Nancy Kelly escapes a terrorist attack at the U.S. consulate in Tunisia, Callen and Sam are sent on a secret and dangerous mission to obtain key evidence from the crime scene.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The Trouble With Harry : An alcoholic ex-fighter (William "The Refrigerator'' Perry) is pursued by a vengeful mobster.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: A Little Town With an Accent : After a mobster threatens to infiltrate a small town, the A-Team comes to the rescue.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The Sound Of Thunder : Gen. Fullbright searches for his Amerasian daughter in Vietnam.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: Dishpan Man : A powerful and mysterious government official (Robert Vaughn) blackmails Hannibal (George Peppard) and his cohorts into cooperating with him on a hostage-freeing mission in Mexico.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: Trial By Fire : The A-Team is convicted of murdering Col. Morrison.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: Firing Line : Murdock escapes from the veterans hospital to save Face, B.A. and Hannibal from execution.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: Quarterback Sneak : The A-Team arranges an exhibition football game to free a famous biochemist in East Berlin. Guest star: Joe Namath.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The Theory of Revolution : The team hopes to rescue American intelligence agents who are imprisoned on an island.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The Say U.N.C.L.E. Affair : Stockwell is kidnapped after ordering the team to confiscate a Russian jet.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: Alive At Five : A reporter attempts to save a future union boss who is a murder target.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: Family Reunion : Stockwell wants the team to reunite a fugitive criminal and his daughter.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: Point Of No Return : The A-Team searches for Hannibal, who disappeared while tracking down stolen plutonium.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The Crystal Skull : Murdock is made a god on an island where the natives mine diamonds.

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The Spy Who Mugged Me : Murdock goes under cover to investigate an assassination plot.

programme.title: The Big Country
programme.desc: The Big Country : Burl Ives earned a Best Supporting Actor Oscar for his portrayal of a family patriarch involved in a bitter land feud in this explosive western drama that also features a sumptuous musical score.

programme.title: Wagon Team
programme.desc: Wagon Team : Gene Autry jails himself to work on the suspected thief of an Army payroll, who escapes, but Gene catches up with him at the medicine show. It's a showdown when Gene goes after the missing payroll.

programme.title: The Shooting
programme.desc: The Shooting : Starring Jack Nicholson ("Chinatown"), a cocky young cowboy sets out to track an experienced ex-bounty hunter who now has a price on his head.

programme.title: Riders In The Sky
programme.desc: Riders In The Sky : Gene Autry helps a gal clear her father's name who is accused of murdering a gambler. When the leader of the gamblers gets involved, it's up to Gene and his comic pal Pat Buttram to outwit the badmen.

programme.title: Barbed Wire
programme.desc: Barbed Wire : In 1878, Gene Autry is a cattle buyer who tries to settle a battle between the cattlemen and homesteaders, when the latter are worked up into a feverish pitch by unscrupulous landowner Steve Rutledge.

programme.title: From Noon Till Three
programme.desc: From Noon Till Three : Charles Bronson plays Graham Dorsey, a bank robber who, through a series of twists and turns finds himself the object of national adoration. A beautiful widow, Amanda Starbuck (Jill Ireland) is responsible for creating this myth.

programme.title: Wild Bill
programme.desc: Wild Bill : Wild Bill focuses on the last days of gunfighter, Wild Bill Hickok, consorting in Deadwood with Calamity Jane, observed by a longtime friend, and haunted by the ghosts of his past, from an old flame to a young man who announces his intention to kill Bill.

programme.title: Billy Two Hats
programme.desc: Billy Two Hats : Archie (Gregory Peck) and his partner, Billy (Desi Arnaz, Jr.) are bandits by trade, staging daring robberies throughout the American frontier. But when their latest heist takes a deadly turn, they are pursued by a ruthless marshal (Jack Warden).

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Bad Dads and Grads : Dan connects with his half-brother but struggles with their father's unequal treatment; Jackie hits it off with a fun married couple.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Beards, Thrupples and Robots : Darlene joins Price Warehouse for income; Jackie advances her relationship with Ron and Janelle; Ben alters his appearance for advertisers.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Tats and Tias : Harris returns home newly-unemployed; Becky seeks childcare; Jackie advises reaching out to Emilio's family; the family ponders tattoos.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: The Icewoman Cometh : Bev returns, wants to spread happiness with her money. She offers to fund Mark's coding camp tuition, but Darlene suggests giving it to Harris.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Pilot Lights & Sister Fights : Dan opens up to Louise; Darlene yearns for motherhood; Ben's responsibilities for Becky interfere with work.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: CPAPs, Hickeys and Biscuits : Dan and Louise deepen their bond amid the challenges of aging; Darlene's night in jail leaves Ben in charge of parenting; Mark's class experiment with a friend results in hickeys.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Bridge Over Troubled Conners : Darlene and Ben hunt for apartments; Jackie, Becky, and Beverly Rose road trip to Mexico to meet her father; Dan confides financial woes to Louise.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Keep on Truckin' Six Feet Apart : The Conners are dealing with the pandemic and life's financial troubles; the Lunch Box is closed for everything but takeout and delivery.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Halloween and the Election vs. the Pandemic : Mary is devastated that Halloween has been canceled by the city, so Darlene and Becky enlist Jackie and the rest of the family to create an unforgettable experience.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Plastics, Trash Talk & Darlene Antoinette : Darlene isolates herself from her co-workers at Wellman Plastics, leaving them with the impression she is an elitist snob.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Birthdays, Babies and Emotional Support Chickens : It's Beverly Rose's first birthday -- but it's not all cake and candles -- when Becky and Emilio disagree on what kind of party they should have.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Friends in High Places and Horse Surgery : Darlene's friendship with a manager at Wellman Plastics lands her in an awkward situation; Louise's brother tries to win over Jackie.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Protest, Drug Test and One Leaves the Nest : Harris's job is in jeopardy after joining a protest she strongly believes in, leading to tense discussions within the family.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: A Cold Mom, a Brother Daddy and a Prison Baby : Ben's mom arrives after an unexpected death and unveils shocking information from the past; Becky and Emilio spend more time together.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Young Love, Old Lions and Middle-Aged Hyenas : Harris brings home her new friend, Josh, leaving the family questioning his intentions; Becky and Ben's attempt to build a closet stirs up family drama.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Promotions, Podcasts and Magic Tea : Darlene earns a promotion at Wellman Plastics, while Ben decides on a new career path; Jackie tries an herbal tea. and it leads to an emotional revelation.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Who Are Bosses, Boats and Eckhart Tolle? : Chuck's new purchase of a boat creates a rift; Darlene hires her first assistant at Wellman's Plastics but soon realizes she is anything but helpful.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Panic Attacks, Hardware Store and Big Mouth Billy Bass : Darlene starts to feel the pressures of her new job; Dan attempts to help Ben out at the hardware store, but the two don't always agree on how to run the business.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: A Stomach Ache, a Heartbreak and a Grave Mistake : Mark is convinced he has COVID-19; Becky and Harris team up to confront Josh, who has been 'ghosting' Harris.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Walden Pond, a Staycation and the Axis Powers : Dan feels helpless when Louise refuses to let him put himself at risk for her; when Jackie learns the competing restaurants are posting negative reviews.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Money, Booze and Lies : Becky meets up with an old friend and feels pressured to impress him; Dan questions his position at the hardware store, leading to a massive fight between him and Ben.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: An Old Dog, New Tricks and a Ticket to Ride : Becky is working double shifts and taking classes at the community college, resulting in less time with her daughter; Dan decides to foster an elderly dog.

programme.title: The Conners

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Regrets, Rehabs and Realtors : Dan encounters a surprise visitor who shares some unsolicited advice, leaving him feeling guilty; Darlene welcomes a new employee to Wellman Plastics.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Cheating, Revelations and a Box of Doll Heads : Mark is stressed and exhausted from studying for an important entrance exam, so Darlene steps in to help, but her plan backfires.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Jeoparde, Sobriete, and Infidelite : Darlene makes a decision about her trip to Hawaii, leading to a heated encounter with Barb; Becky gives a presentation on addiction to Mark's class.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Two Proposals, a Homecoming and a Bear : Two proposals bring big changes to the Conner family; Jackie uses her Jeopardy infamy to her advantage.

programme.title: The Conners

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Education, Corruption and Damnation : After Ben declines Darlene's proposal, she starts to ponder why she tends to be so negative in life, prompting her to start a journey of self-discovery, which leads to Pastor Phil.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Sober Sex, Plastic Silverware and Losing My Religion : Part of Becky's recovery program mandates she make amends with people she has lied to over the years, leading her to meet up again with a former high school friend; Darlene visits with Pastor Phil; Louise continues planning her wedding.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: The Wedding of Dan and Louise : It's Dan and Louise's wedding day, but it's a far-from-perfect walk down the aisle, especially when a weather report forecasts that a tornado is headed for Lanford creating chaos and surprises.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Peter Pan, The Backup Plan, Adventures in Babysitting and A River Runs Through It : The Conner home is decked out for Halloween, despite the leak in the roof that forces Dan and Louise to cancel their honeymoon; Becky surprises the newlyweds and takes them on a virtual adventure around the world.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Young Love, Old Love and Take This Job and Shove It : Darlene worries about Mark making friends at his new school; Mark turns to Ben for personal advice; Becky finds herself thriving in college and starts thinking about her future plans.

programme.title: Pokemon the Series

programme.title: Pokemon the Series
programme.desc: Go Go Ludicolo! : Ash's Corphish is having a hard time with all the attention given to the newly evolved Grovyle.

programme.title: Pokemon the Series

programme.title: Pokemon the Series

programme.title: Pokemon the Series
programme.desc: Balance of Power : Ash has challenged Max and May's father, Norman, to a battle before—but this time, it's for a Gym badge!

programme.title: Pokemon the Series
programme.desc: A Six Pack Attack! : When Professor Oak makes a surprise appearance in Hoenn, bringing along Bulbasaur, Charmander, and Squirtle, Ash joins forces with them to battle Team Rocket.

programme.title: Pokemon the Series
programme.desc: The Bicker the Better : When another pair of Trainers challenge Ash and May to a Tag Battle, a falling-out between the two friends causes them to lose—but they soon resolve their differences.

programme.title: Pokemon the Series
programme.desc: Grass Hysteria! : When May accidentally enters a forbidden spot where only Grass-type Pokémon live, she quickly befriends a Bulbasaur.

programme.title: Pokemon the Series

programme.title: Pokemon the Series
programme.desc: Whiscash and Ash : When a famed Whiscash swallows Ash's Gym badges, he teams up with a fisherman to try to reel it in!

programme.title: Pokemon the Series

programme.title: Pokemon the Series

programme.title: Pokemon the Series
programme.desc: Cruisin’ for a Losin’ : May decides to use Bulbasaur in the Rubello Town Contest. Will its lack of experience cost her a ribbon?

programme.title: Pokemon the Series

programme.title: Pokemon the Series

programme.desc: A Shroomish Skirmish : Team Rocket infuriates a group of Breloom, forcing Ash and friends to flee from their wrath.

programme.title: Pokemon the Series

programme.title: Pokemon the Series

programme.title: Pokemon the Series

programme.title: Pokemon the Series
programme.desc: Crazy as a Lunatone : A group of conspiracy theorists known as the Pokémon Mystery Club pursues a Lunatone suspected to be behind a recent series of strange events.

programme.title: Pokemon the Series

programme.title: Pokemon the Series

programme.title: Pokemon the Series
programme.desc: Pokéblock, Stock and Berry : Before taking part in the Lilycove Pokémon Contest, May has time to study the art of Pokéblock preparation with a local master.

programme.desc: Judgment Day! : Trying to get to Mossdeep City for Ash's next Gym battle, our heroes are forced to take a detour.

programme.title: Pokemon the Series
programme.desc: Clamperl of Wisdom : Our heroes reconnect with an unlucky Spoink, and it's still having trouble holding onto its pearl!
